74071.subject: BitMine Brings Strategy's STRC Playbook to an ETH Treasury
74071.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

These are today’s biggest crypto and DeFi news:

* **[BitMine](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/bitmine-files-300m-preferred-stock-offering-9-5-yield-eth-treasury)**[ files $300M preferred stock ](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/bitmine-files-300m-preferred-stock-offering-9-5-yield-eth-treasury)

* **[Coinbase International](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/coinbase-launches-pre-ipo-perpetual-futures-spacex)**[ opens USDC-settled pre-IPO perps](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/coinbase-launches-pre-ipo-perpetual-futures-spacex)

* **[Sky](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/sky-launches-fixed-rate-yield-product-built-on-pendle-targeting-6b-susds-pool)**[ launches Pendle-built fixed-rate yield ](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/sky-launches-fixed-rate-yield-product-built-on-pendle-targeting-6b-susds-pool)

* **[PENDLE](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/pendle-token-goes-live-on-revolut-reaching-20m-eea-crypto-traders)**[ goes live on Revolut](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/pendle-token-goes-live-on-revolut-reaching-20m-eea-crypto-traders)

* **[Bitcoin ETFs](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/spot-bitcoin-etf-outflow-streak-record-13-days)**[ extend record outflow streak ](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/spot-bitcoin-etf-outflow-streak-record-13-days)

**[BitMine](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/bitmine-files-300m-preferred-stock-offering-9-5-yield-eth-treasury)**, the Ethereum-treasury company sitting on $9.2 billion in unrealized losses, filed a prospectus supplement for up to $300 million in Series A Perpetual Preferred Stock at a 9.5% cumulative dividend. Tom Lee is trying to apply [Strategy's STRC playbook](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/strategy-sells-32-bitcoin-fund-preferred-dividends-first-sale-since-2022) to an ETH instrument. 

Meanwhile, [Coinbase International](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/coinbase-launches-pre-ipo-perpetual-futures-spacex) opened USDC-settled pre-IPO perpetual futures with SpaceX as the first listing, [Sky shipped a Pendle-built fixed-rate yield product](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/sky-launches-fixed-rate-yield-product-built-on-pendle-targeting-6b-susds-pool) aimed at its $6 billion sUSDS pool, and [PENDLE itself went live on Revolut](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/pendle-token-goes-live-on-revolut-reaching-20m-eea-crypto-traders) for 20 million EEA crypto traders.

The counter signal is significant: [spot Bitcoin ETFs](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/spot-bitcoin-etf-outflow-streak-record-13-days) extended their outflow streak to a record 13 consecutive days this week, $4.4 billion cumulative since May 15. That’s the largest withdrawal run in the products' history. Strategy's [Michael Saylor](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/saylor-bitcoin-ai-capital-rotation-strategy-paper-loss), sitting on a $10.3 billion paper loss of his own, said the slide was 'AI capital rotation.’ 

Spot allocators are stepping back at the same time tre

74070.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/666ae944-5cae-4c6c-abd6-970020388daf/BD-Banner-Sponsor_Template.png?t=1780094904)
Follow image link: (https://peaq.xyz)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6ae28dab-3a36-440c-bc79-2e2a69fde017/123.png?t=1780609801)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bdac109-1964-46fb-8111-5557499f9521/unnamed.jpg?t=1780609723)
Caption: 

# Thursday links: Crypto success stories

[Crypto makes AI private](https://x.com/YanLiberman/status/2056797867415916997)

Delphi co-founder Yan Liberman details the investing case for a much-needed crypto success story: Venice AI.

It’s an asset-lite business: It doesn’t own any GPUs or develop any models. Instead, Venice offers access to open-source LLMs and makes money by marking up the inference it buys from hyperscalers and sells to users.

Users pay the mark-up because Venice enables them to use the models privately. For the more technically minded, Venice offers “anonymous proxying, open-source model routing, hardware-attested TEE inference, and end-to-end encrypted inference.” 

Liberman says this makes Venice the “most complete privacy solution in the AI market today.”

Note that he didn’t say the _crypto_-AI market. He said the AI market. 

Venice is a rare case of crypto tokenomics enabling something unique — and genuinely useful — in a market that otherwise has nothing to do with crypto.

Also rare for crypto: Its token is reasonably priced. 

Liberman does a lot of work to estimate that Venice is currently on pace to hit $260 million of ARR a year from now. On that basis, the VVV token — pseudo-equity in Venice — is currently valued at about 3.2x forward revenue.

That seems unusually reasonable considering the current growth rate: Subscribers grew 50% over just the last three months. Better yet, token usage tripled over the same period.

It’s funny — and perhaps telling — that one of crypto’s most substantial projects ever gets one of crypto’s most modest valuations ever.

That’s not to say it’s obviously cheap. Liberman walks us through a long list of risks to the investment case, including competitive threats from every direction. 

But it’s already quite the success story.

[Crypto makes finance anonymous?](https://www.wsj.com/finance/currencies/zcash-crypto-winklevoss-78d71d51?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

==Crypto’s most cypherpunk project has earned a profile in the epitome-of-establishment ====_Wall Street Journal_====. Zcash is a “more secret version of Bitcoin,” the headline reads, “and it's on a tear.”==

==Indeed. The token is up about 10x since crypto people renewed their enthusiasm for privacy less than a year ago.==

==Bitcoin is down ab

74069.body:
Forbes P.M.

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення

Good evening, Drug discovery is one of the great promises of AI, and newcomer Chai Discovery has catapulted itself to the front of the race. In January, the startup—newly valued at $1.3 billion—announced a deal with Eli Lilly. Now, it has signed another big AI drug development partnership, this time with $63 billion (2025 revenue) Pfizer. Learn more about Chai Discovery and why big players are endorsing it here. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Getty Images These States Are Winning The Race For Federal AI Education Funding New analysis suggests that federal funding supporting AI-related education is increasingly concentrated in just a handful of states. 74065.subject: 🔷 The Daily: 'The low is almost in'
74065.body:
Polymarket's UMA vote upholds 'No' outcome on Strategy bitcoin sale market despite backlash | Bitmine to launch STRC-style preferred stock offering amid $9.2 billion unrealized ETH losses | Coinbase launches pre-IPO perpetual futures, starting with SpaceX | Arthur Hayes sells entire HYPE and NEAR positions, citing AI IPOs and US midterms |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Thursday! *Bitcoin's* 14% slide over the past week has prompted analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403659/the-rally-that-wasnt-bitcoin-slides-14-in-one-week-as-etf-outflows-strategy-sale-and-oil-prices-hit-sentiment?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) to dub its prior move "the rally that wasn't," as a rare *Strategy* sale, a $4.4 billion spot ETF outflow streak, and a tougher macro backdrop combined to sour sentiment and push the cryptocurrency to its lowest level in four months.

In today's newsletter, *Standard Chartered* says the *bitcoin* bottom is near, *Polymarket's UMA* vote upholds a "No" outcome on *Strategy's bitcoin* sale market, *Bitmine* is set to launch an *STRC* -style preferred stock offering, and more.

Meanwhile, *JPMorgan* said ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403676/jpmorgan-crypto-bill-narrow-window-passage-this-year?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) the crypto market structure bill may have only a narrow window for passage this year.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*📉 'The low is almost in': Standard Chartered says bitcoin bottom near after tough week for crypto*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/403625/the-low-is-almost-in-standard-chartered-says-bitcoin-bottom-near-after-tough-week-for-crypto?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Standard Chartered's Geoffrey Kendrick* said a *bitcoin* bottom is "almost in," arguing that resilient spot ETF holdings and a likely *Strategy* buyback suggest the worst of the sell-off may be over.

* Kendrick now views current levels as a "buying zone," despite bitcoin falling more than 22% over the past month and trading around $63,000.
* The analyst said a key reason for his change in outlook since a $50,000 bitcoin call back in February is that spot bitcoin ETF holdings

74061.subject: American capitalism has taken an apocalyptic turn
74061.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTd9AAcAAAAAAkfeuNGp1PfXQiZIH_frGHdYfYtyQZ3NQemoU1TdfPjlO8Mr-pISvRBMBc83WmM5eBR9N7q7xWaM5SjBv11B0YPaUCZyaJbA_yYjIpUJIyvz 

June 4th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTd9AAcAAAAAAkfeuNGqNW8zUdTYerUo2PMYWbBEzkughTut1hzJ1LUAm6gkMUMN_4PKtAHHp8XQvKzRWdjM_37gfSCAWey3gCouLUzPZfV94rcS19WbP9M7 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTd9AAcAAAAAAkfeuNGrzm6tQSR8cg_xvOT9WV5WYnqn_f7TemE38wrpxlBku569qoj-Y6E1b0lLwfR9cjMl2LzdLrQ5dZNjklOKVa3p8YikOBzL6PNVe5Cg 



 
 

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 “America is an experiment in terrifying yourself to riches,” reads an intriguing sentence in this week’s Schumpeter column. 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTd9AAcAAAAAAkfeuNGs6Squ8iplqwWaEvTh5PVI1dd6sV9ao_AsRhUJBUBCwbhqHZJfPu_H8liMk455aKLoyhY2OxtwqDbklrptg5V3U5i7A9Xanys 
Our columnist 
 notices that a lot of what’s driving the investment frenzy in sectors like artificial intelligence and defence is an apocalyptic view of the way the world is heading. Will investors profit from cataclysm or be overwhelmed by it?


 A murder in Southampton last year has become a political controversy that compounds the tragedy. There are questions to answer about how the police responded to the stabbing of a y
